step_by_step_thinking:
For discharge criteria after Dengue hemorrhagic shock, we look for indicators showing stable vital signs and adequate hydration. Urine output greater than 200 ml indicates proper renal function and rehydration, suggesting readiness for discharge.
\boxed{B} Based on the available information focusing on urinary output as an indicator of renal function and hydration, B appears to align best with established discharge criteria post-shock.
